cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why weekend afternoons would become dead time without sports programs. Let me read through the passage again to find the relevant part. The passage says, "Sport events are the most popular weekend programs, especially among male viewers who may not watch much television at other times during the week. This means the television networks are able to sell advertising time at relatively high prices during what normally would be dead time for programming." So, the key point here is that without sports, the weekend afternoons would be dead time. The reason given is that sports attract viewers who don't watch much TV otherwise. So, if there were no sports, there wouldn't be enough viewers during those times. Let me check the answer options. Option A says "Because there would be few viewers." That seems to align with the passage. The passage implies that sports bring in viewers during those times when normally there are few. The other options don't seem to fit. Option
